characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: The main robot itself. Originally the Avatar Prototype, a weapon created by the Glorft, until captured by the humans and refitted into the M.E.G.A.S. (Mecha Earth Guard Attack System.) It was supposed to be sent two years in the past to provide the edge humanity needed in the war against the Glorft. However, an attack from the aliens sent the mech back to the early 20th century instead, and it stayed there for decades until Coop unearthed it in a Jersey City junkyard. The...extensivemodifications he made to Megas serve him well in the ensuing conflicts against the Glorft. Awesome, but Impractical: Manual mode. While it does sound badass to be able to control Megas via your own body movements, it cannot use its weapons and its limited by Coop's lack of stamina. There's a reason why Coop installed it as a backup. Bigger on the Inside: Aside from itsHammerspaceArsenal, the interior size of its chassis is often exaggerated. Combining Mecha: Something of a parody of one. Coop's muscle car attaches to the robot body to control it as a head, but can still operate as a normal car without it. The parody is taken further when Coop uses the S-Force's Zorps to give MEGAS a power boost... which just amounts to shoving the big thing's arms and legs into the Zorps' rear ends to wear them as boots and gauntlets, and sticking the bird robot onto its back. Do-Anything Robot: Exaggerated as part of the overallAffectionate Parody. MEGAS can do just about anything Coop desires in a given episode.Wave-Motion Gun? You got it. Rockets and drills? Yep. Becoming an impromptuMotion-Capture Mechausing a DDR dance pad? It can do that too. It's allPlayed for Laughs.

characters.name: MEGAS
characters.description: The main robot itself. Originally the Avatar Prototype, a weapon created by the Glorft, until captured by the humans and refitted into the M.E.G.A.S. (Mecha Earth Guard Attack System.) It was supposed to be sent two years in the past to provide the edge humanity needed in the war against the Glorft. However, an attack from the aliens sent the mech back to the early 20th century instead, and it stayed there for decades until Coop unearthed it in a Jersey City junkyard. The...extensivemodifications he made to Megas serve him well in the ensuing conflicts against the Glorft. Awesome, but Impractical: Manual mode. While it does sound badass to be able to control Megas via your own body movements, it cannot use its weapons and its limited by Coop's lack of stamina. There's a reason why Coop installed it as a backup. Bigger on the Inside: Aside from itsHammerspaceArsenal, the interior size of its chassis is often exaggerated. Combining Mecha: Something of a parody of one. Coop's muscle car attaches to the robot body to control it as a head, but can still operate as a normal car without it. The parody is taken further when Coop uses the S-Force's Zorps to give MEGAS a power boost... which just amounts to shoving the big thing's arms and legs into the Zorps' rear ends to wear them as boots and gauntlets, and sticking the bird robot onto its back. Do-Anything Robot: Exaggerated as part of the overallAffectionate Parody. MEGAS can do just about anything Coop desires in a given episode.Wave-Motion Gun? You got it. Rockets and drills? Yep. Becoming an impromptuMotion-Capture Mechausing a DDR dance pad? It can do that too. It's allPlayed for Laughs.

characters.name: "Coop" Cooplowski
characters.description: Coop is Megas's current owner, a seasoned mechanic, gamer and foodie. He lives in his mother's basement in Jersey City, and is a fan of Pay-Per-View wrestling and cheesy monster movies. Achievements in Ignorance: Coop is an amazing mechanic but sometimes even he has no idea what his killer weapons do. There's also the fact that he managed to get the robot working at all despite replacing the control hub with a car and wiring the control layout with multiple videogame controllers that he constantly tinkers and upgrades with. Acrofatic: His reflexes are surprisingly good for someone his size, though that's not to say he enjoys strenuous exercise, or can do so for very long.Coop:Runnin's for chumps! Arson, Murder, and Jaywalking: During the final confrontation with every monster he fights, he will typically list the three most terrible things the bad guy did to him. The third one is always the most trivial, like complaining about the antagonist making him jog. Attack! Attack! Attack!: Coop's main strategy when fighting often involves him simply beating the ever loving tar out of anything against him, or, alternatively, the biggest explosion he can find.Deconstructedin the finalewhere Coop's usual strategy gets Megas destroyed and he's forced to think outside of the box.

characters.name: Jamie
characters.description: Relatively harmless slacker who regularly sleeps on Coop's couch and camps on MEGAS' backseat, whining. Has occasional milliseconds of bravery and intelligence, but mostly just acts as straight-man to the insanity of his friend's robot piloting adventures. Be Careful What You Wish For: In "Junk in the trunk" he complains about the team always getting attacked by "slobbery monsters" and wishes for once they could be attacked by "a giant hot chick". Guess what happens in later episode "Ultra Chicks"? Butt-Monkey: He is the prime target of mockery and physical abuse in the show. Catchphrase: Usually drones "Nice..." when he finds amusement in something. Cowardly Sidekick: Whenever there is a bad guy around, he will scream in terror while Coop and Kiva work to solve the problem.Jamie: I'm too pretty to die!

characters.name: Commander Kiva Andru, Earth Coalition Space Force
characters.description: Time-traveling Ace test pilot from the year 3037 and MEGAS' original pilot. When Earth fell to the Glorft, she and MEGAS were sent back to the 21st century in order to prepare the world for the inevitable conflict. However, upon arriving in 2004, she found that MEGAS had arrived seven decades earlier, spent much of the interval rusting in a Jersey junkyard - and was ultimately salvaged and modified beyond her capability to pilot by Coop. Making the best of an insane situation, she reluctantly assumed the role of Coop's tactical adviser against the Glorft. Actor Allusion: It's not the first time Wendee Lee voiced a redhead soldier who's supposed to protect aSuper Prototypemech and interacts with a blonde man in her life, a nod to Christina Mackenzie fromMobile Suit Gundam 0080: War in the Pocket. Ace Pilot: She is quite seasoned from battles against Glorft, both for Earth Coalition and the resistance, which made her as the only one withexperience and rationalityfor Coop. Unfortunately for her, she rarely gets to display her skill thanks to Coop rendering MEGAS inoperable to her. Though one episode "The Return" shows her defeating a Glorft robot with MEGAS, so she hassomelevel of competency with it—-just not as much as Coop. Action Girl: She can beat anything short of an opponent that requires Megas to defeat. Affectionate Nickname: Coop calls her "Red".

characters.name: Warmaster Gorrath
characters.description: The Warmaster of the Glorft who destroyed the Earth Coalition at the beginning of the series, and attempts to wipe out the remnants with the Avatar mech as "humanity's coup de grace" but Kiva steals it and modified into Megas. He is determined to get his machine back, even more so when he and his forces of theKarajorwere transported back to the past and end up stuck, needing the Time Warp of Megas (unbeknownst to him, Coop trashed the device) to do so. Big Bad: He is the show's primary source of conflict. Brought Down to Badass: Is a capable fighter on par with Kiva even on occasions where he's without his giant robot. The Caligula: He was originally a pretty effective (if still mean) leader, but repeated defeats at the hands of Coop had instilled a virulent hatred into Gorrath such that he prioritizes destroying him even at the expense of the big picture. Cold-Blooded Torture: In "The Driver’s Seat", he tortures Coop to make him tell how to operate Megas. This isPlayed for Laughs, as he tortures Coop by smashing a slushy with his fist right in front of Coop, and taking a bite from a Philly cheesesteak in front of Coop.

characters.name: Glorft Commander
characters.description: The second-in-command of the Glorft forces serving Warmaster Gorrath. Butt-Monkey: Best seen at the end of "Terminate Her" where Gorrath uses him as an impromptu punching bag whilst he's using a hologram to make himself look like Coop. Captain Obvious: Does this on occasion to the point of annoying Gorrath.Gorrath: Your penchant for stating the obvious is getting old, commander. Deadpan Snarker: Occasionally does this out of Gorrath's earshot.Gorrath: This plan of yours have better succeed, commander. Or I'll have your jhor-blocks. I have no patience for failure.Commander: Unless it's your own. The Dissenter Is Always Right: His objections to Gorrath's plans usually end up being proven right even though he never receives credit for it.

characters.name: Mecha-MEGAS
characters.description: A parody of Mecha Godzilla. A modified duplicate of MEGAS created by the Glorft and piloted by Warmaster Gorrath. Big Badass Rig: Its head is resembles a big rig truck. Evil Knockoff: To MEGAS, complete with a set of Glorft-stylized "mud-flaps" on the legs. Fire-Breathing Weapon: Can breathe fire from its head.

characters.name: Goat
characters.description: Between catching a tan on a scrap hill or relaxing in his favourite lawn chair, Goat is the owner and operator of the junkyard in which Coop found MEGAS. Like Jamie, he tries to hit on girls (usually Kiva) and falls just a bit short. Abhorrent Admirer: He has a crush on Kiva, but she can’t stand him. Brick Joke: In "Thanksgiving Throwdown", he is seen unsuccessfully trying to flirt with a girl by offering to buy her a churro. Later on in the episode, we see that she actually he accepted his offer. Casanova Wannabe: Zigzagged. Most of the time his efforts to impress women (especially Kiva) fail, however he has been successful a few times. Character as Himself: While Scot Rienecker was credited as voicing Goat in the first season's end credits, the end credits for the second season instead had Goat listed as playing himself.

characters.name: The S-Force
characters.description: A group of young superheroes from the planet Sarus. The S-Force protects beings of other galaxies from evil forces. They first suspected Coop to be a bad guy most likely due to Coop's senseless destruction on Earth, only to learn that he wasn't evil, just clumsy. Despite this they became great allies of the heroes. Ain't Too Proud to Beg: Targon detests Coop, deeming him a bad influence. Nonetheless, he agrees to Jax's idea of recruiting him for help when the S-Force are captured. Animal Mecha: Keeping with their Power Ranger parody, their individual mechs are an eagle, a rhino, a gorilla, a panther and a kangaroo. Badass in Distress: The plot of their second appearance; all but Jax are being held captive by Zarek in "S-Force S.O.S." Big Eater: Mac tends to eat a lot, but he's still nothing in the huge appetite department when compared to Coop. Though thanks to Coop's "training," in S-Force's next appearance Mac's now almost as big as Coop. Sloan also starts to fatten up at the end of the episode, though is back to his smaller size when the team reappears.

characters.name: Little Tommy
characters.description: A kid who lives in Jersey City, he often says "Cooool." when he sees something unusual, such as an alien appearing in the middle of a baseball game. He usually has a Slushie in his hand and delivers the line after taking a long sip. The Cameo: Usually appears spectating Coop's wacky hinjinks from afar. Catchphrase: He says a drawn-out "Cooool" whenever he sees something unusual. Unwitting Instigator of Doom: Subverted thanks to the show's cancellation, but he takes a piece of the destroyed R.E.G.I.S. Mark 5 after its destruction as a souvenir. Given its ability to regenerateFrom a Single Cell, it would've likely made a comeback thanks to this.

characters.name: Tiny
characters.description: A friend of Coop.Despite his name, Tiny is considerably larger than Coop. Though like Coop, he is also fond of working with cars. Bald of Authority: He's bald and in "The Fat And The Furious", he leads the humans in their cars against the Glorft. Cool Car: His deserves special mention in being able to go toe-to-toe withMEGASin a car competition. Friendly Rivalry: He and Coop compete in car shows but are still great friends. Ironic Name: Despite his name, is very large and tall.

characters.name: Georgie
characters.description: A video game expert much like Coop, only he isn't as good as him. He also seems to be fond of theLurpsand plays their game series, which is apparently the only game series Coop never played. Black and Nerdy: He plays this trope straight. Defeat Equals Friendship: Despite their initial conflict, him and Coop become good friends afterwards. Expy: The Lurps are obviously a parody ofThe Smurfs. Pyrrhic Victory: Proves that he's played more video games than Coop but then again, the only game Coop hasn't played isLove those Lurps 7of all things...

characters.name: Gina
characters.description: A girl Jamie tried to date, she thought he had a nice car (which was actually Coop's). So Jamie lied to get her to go for a ride which ended up turning into a cat and mouse chase with the Glorft until Coop regained control of MEGAS. All Girls Want Bad Boys: She finds Jamie's quirks kinda endearing. Early-Bird Cameo: She's the same girl Fruity was flirting with on the subway inDowntown. Muggle: Is unaware of who the Glorft are and doesn't want to find out. One-Shot Character: She only appears in "Dude, Where’s My Head?". She probably decided to stay away from Jamie after their date ended up being a near-death experience.

characters.name: Cal
characters.description: The owner of the impound lot that MEGAS's head-car was towed to at one point. Coop is apparently such a frequent flyer of the lot that Cal knows him by name. No Hero Discount: Even though Coop urges him to release his car despite his expired license because of a dangerous alien was attacking, he insists that he can't because of the rules. One-Shot Character: He only appears in "DMV - Department of Megas Violations". Unusually Uninteresting Sight: Is quite lax about the whole giant robot appearances.

characters.name: Florxin and Tolbit
characters.description: Crab-like aliens who perform transporting and clean-up tasks. Florxin is the tall cynical one and Tolbit is the short clueless one. Big Eater: Tolbit usually has his mind on food. According to Florxin, it was his idea to stop at Earth for a snack while they were transporting dangerous cargo. The Cameo: They make a brief appearance in the ending credits of "Junk in the Trunk". Heterosexual Life-Partners: They're very close. Quirky Janitor: They are essentially cosmic janitors.

characters.name: Ally
characters.description: Kiva's ancestor from the past, sharing similar appearances. Gorrath tried to kill her, which would directly erase Kiva from history, preventing MEGAS from being sent to the present. Come with Me If You Want to Live: Kiva tries pulling this trope on her but she's distrustful until Jamie reminds her about the aliens hunting her. Damsel in Distress: Coop, Kiva, and Jamie have to prevent her from being killed by the Glorft. One-Shot Character: She only appears in "Terminate Her", sinceshe decided to leave townafter the Glorft attacked her. Screw This, I'm Outta Here!: Decides to leave New Jersey and return to Brooklyn so that the Glorft won't find her.

characters.name: Chieftain Ator and the Cryocks
characters.description: The chieftain of the Cryocks i.e. "Space Bigfoots". He is the leader of his tribe of Cryocks who lived in an ice village. His village was protected by a defector of the Cyrellian army but Coop mistook him for the enemy. Ator then explained this to Coop and Coop in return became their guardian... for a short while. Aliens Speaking English: Despite being aliens, they speak perfect English. Defiant to the End: Refused to go down without a fight even though they knew they stood little chance. Destructive Saviour: Coop becomes this for them. Drallag evenLampshadeshow he did more damage than him. Fur Bikini: After the the ice caps melt turning the planet tropical, some of the female Cryocks can be seen wearing fur bikinis to adjust to the new climate.

characters.name: Guardian of the Cryocks
characters.description: The protector of the Cryocks who ensure the planet's protection from his former comrades, the Cyrellians. Unfortunately, he makes the mistake of picking a fight with Coop and loses his life, allowing Drallag to take opportunity of mobilizing and extract all of the ice from the world at last. Arm Cannon: He wields this when protecting the Cryocks' planet from the Cyrellians, and uses it against Coop in battle. Bullying a Dragon: He picks a fight with Coop and his friends, believing them to be invaders and attacked, only to lose his life. Defector from Decadence: He used to be part of Drallag's army until he turned his guns against them to protect the native race of the icy world. Expy: As the Cyrellians were based off from the Decepticons that seek to drain everything for power, he is this to the Autobots that protect the organics from their enemies. In fact, his robot design isa fusion of Hound, Trailbreaker and Warpath.

characters.name: Coop's Mom
characters.description: Coop's mom who can occasionally be heard yelling at Coop, usually asking what he wants to eat or to pay a bill. Disappeared Dad: Coop's father is never heard or mentioned. The Voice: She never appears on screen and is only heard. Uncertain Doom: Her counterpart's fate in Evil Coop's dimension was unknown as while the house and garage that contains an abandoned Megas inside remain intact, she was nowhere to be found.

characters.name: Skippy
characters.description: Coop's annoying bratty cousin. Coop was forced to babysit him in an episode while his mother and Aunt Ester went shopping in the city. Bratty Half-Pint: He's a pretty obnoxious kid. One-Shot Character: He only appears in "Don’t Tell Mom the Babysitter’s Coop". Tag Along Kid: He goes with the heroes on their adventure against their wishes. Unusually Uninteresting Sight: Zig-zagged. Riding in a giant robot wasn't enough to impress him, but seeing the Glorft Moonbase was deemed cool by his standards.

characters.name: The Ultra-Cadets
characters.description: Space warriors who came to Earth looking for help, but instead they mistook Jamie for Coop. They needed Coop's assistance in fighting the Kurdock, so Jamie went instead, not knowing what he was getting himself into. Badass in Distress: Their combined might allows them to fight evenly with Megas but even they could not defeat the Kurdock. Head Blast: Galaxia's ponytail can be become a gun in robot mode. Heart Beat-Down: Nova can fire heart-shaped energy blasts. One-Shot Character: They only appear in "Ultra Chicks".

characters.name: Magnanimous
characters.description: The chairman of the Galactic Combat Championship Federation. He uses Coop to make money in his battle arena, but when Coop refuses to throw a fight for him, Magnanimous uses booby traps and even threatens his friends to make him cooperate. Advertised Extra: Despite appearing in the show’s intro, he only appears in "Battle Royale" and "The Return". And Show It to You: Coop defeats his Mech-Naninmous robot by ripping its heart out which wasMagnanimous himself. Cephalothorax: Being a reference to M.O.D.O.K., he looks like a giant floating head with disproportionately tiny limbs, which Jamie lampshades when he first sees him.Jamie:Hey, guys, whaddya call a big head with tiny arms and legs. Chainsaw Good: His second mech.He makes good use of it against T-botthough he doesn't have the same luck against Coop.

characters.name: R.E.G.I.S. Mark  5
characters.description: An advanced and powerful alien robot gone horribly awry, it was supposed to be dumped into a black hole by a cloaked ship flown by Florxin and Tolbit. Unfortunately, Coop damaged the ship and the R.E.G.I.S landed on Earth. Achilles' Heel: Requires solar power to function. Without a constant supply it becomes useless until it is exposed to sun light again. Affably Evil: Despite actively trying to eliminate everyone and everything its mannerisms are rather polite even when threatening others.R.E.G.I.S.: Surrender now, and I will destroy you peacefully. Asteroids Monster: Coop's attempts to smash it eventually lead to it splitting into a small army of itself thatspeak in unison. Badass Boast: Loves to do this, and frequently gets beaten to a pulp while in the middle of a monologue.

characters.name: Robotic Worms
characters.description: Giant silicon-based biomechencial worms that are attracted to and feed on pure energy. They were living on a deserted ring-world that was actually an interstellar data storage drive. Energy Absorption: Their instinctual drive. Monster of the Week: They only appear in "Buggin’ the System". Reflectionless Useless Eyes: They have eyes but can only see energy. Averted with the Queen worm. Silicon-Based Life: Though they are apparently able to reproduce organically.

characters.name: Planet-Eating Monster
characters.description: Planet-Eating Monster: A planet-sized space monster that feeds on radio and television waves. Coop accidentally transmitted a powerful, hi-frequency radio wave into space which caught the monster's attention when Coop tried to "juice" his satellite dish to boost his television reception. After Jamie suggested that the satellite was down, the trio found the monster in space eating TV satellites. Big Dumb Object: It's a giant entity from space that causes the conflict in its single episode. Feed It a Bomb: Coop blows it up with a EMP warhead accidentally filled withsoda and pop rocks. Genius Loci: The creature is basically a giant living planet. Monster of the Week: It only appears in "TV Dinner".

characters.name: Grrkek
characters.description: A giant space alien that can kill planets who cares for nothing but destruction and misery. He was sealed away long ago, but was released by Coop when he was testing a new game, not knowing it was a containment cartridge. Advertised Extra: He’s one of the villains who appears in the show's intro, but is only aMonster of the Week. Atomic Superpower: Has the ability to generate fusion reactions powerful enough to devastate planets. Badass Boast: "You will rue the day you met Grrkek the Planet Killer." Blood Knight: Craves nothing but destruction. His only regret is that he can only destroy something once.

characters.name: Ender
characters.description: One of the S-Force's enemies. He "ends things. People, planets, galaxies". Ender appeared while Coop was fighting the S-Force and wanted to partner with Coop to rule the universe together. But, when Coop insisted he was good and refused Ender's offer, he instead fought with Coop. Advertised Extra: He’s one of the villains who appears in the show's intro, but is only aMonster of the Week. Badass Boast: "I'm Ender. I end things. People, planets, galaxies!" Blood Knight: If his name didn't tip you off, helivesfor destroying and killing everything he can. Card-Carrying Villain: He openly refers to himself as evil, admires Coop after mistaking him for a villain, and seems to enjoy evil for the sake of it.

characters.name: Varsin
characters.description: A redneck-type alien who owned a junk planet. He allowed Coop and his friends to look for a photonic stabilizer only so he could take MEGAS and steal the time drive. He pilots a monstrous robot known as the Franken. Bitch in Sheep's Clothing: At first seems like a helpful junkhound until he finds out Megas has a timedrive. The Collector: The main reason he wanted the Time Drive is so he could go back in time and double his collection. Fish Eyes: It getslampshadedby Coop.Coop:(to Kiva)Did you notice that guy Varsin’s eyes? They look off in two different directions. Creepy! Gadgeteer Genius: He’s good at building robots, and he claims he can fixanything.

characters.name: Darklos
characters.description: A space alien bounty hunter chick who was wanted in several nebulas for kidnapping and information trafficking. She was ordered to find and capture Kiva Andru so her clients could extract information from her brain. The Beastmaster: Imprisons a race of giant Rhino-like aliens to serve as her minions. Bounty Hunter: Contracted to hunt down Kiva. Dark Action Girl: Being a ruthless bounty hunter who enslaved a race of aliens to ride into battle and is willing to harvest Kiva's still-living brain, she certainly qualifies. Dreadlock Warrior: Her white hair is styled like dreadlocks.

characters.name: Halcyon Knights
characters.description: Robot warriors ofThe Empireof the Halcyon Worlds. The only named robots are Zanzor and Gyvven. When Coop accidentally destroyed the Flame of Azeroth, their sacred treasure, the Emperor issued a challenge to the robots who were competing to claim his throne: bring him the robot who destroyed the flame to face judgment Asshole Victim:Zanzoar ends up on the receiving end of this by Coop who takes the Metal Maiden sword and kills him with it. No one mourns for Zanzoar and they want to take down Megas to be new emperor before they stood down when Coop uses flaming hands that corresponds with the prophecy.Downplayed. Even if Coop didn't accidentally destroy their Flame of Azeroth that set off the episode's plot, the Halcyon Knights are still brutal conquerors that subjugated worlds and their destruction when Coop's attempt to reignite the Flame of Azeroth that backfired is well-deserved. Zanzoar ends up on the receiving end of this by Coop who takes the Metal Maiden sword and kills him with it. No one mourns for Zanzoar and they want to take down Megas to be new emperor before they stood down when Coop uses flaming hands that corresponds with the prophecy. Downplayed. Even if Coop didn't accidentally destroy their Flame of Azeroth that set off the episode's plot, the Halcyon Knights are still brutal conquerors that subjugated worlds and their destruction when Coop's attempt to reignite the Flame of Azeroth that backfired is well-deserved. Blood Knight: Their entire race loves to fight and they were known to have fought giant monsters and subjugate entire worlds.

characters.name: Kurdock
characters.description: A giant, fire-breathing monster that has been terrorizing the planet of the Ultra-Cadets. The Ultra-Cadets searched for Coop to ask for his help to save their planet and Jamie foolishly pretending to be Coop to try to get a date went with them by mistake. In their fight, Jamie comically ran from the monster and led it to the foot of a volcano, where it accidentally slipped off before it could finish Jamie. Domed Hometown: The Ultra Cadet's home employs a dome as a result of the Kurdock. Monster of the Week: It only appears in "Ultra Chicks". Nice Job Breaking It, Hero!: Jamie's actions only cause the Kurdock to come back from the volcano twice as strong, and this time, the Ultra Cadet's home city is without its shield. Twisted Echo Cut: After sending Jamie off to fight the Kurdock, the Ultra-Cadets joke that he's probably making the Kurdock beg to be spared. Then we cut to see Jamie begging the Kurdock to sparehim.

characters.name: T-Bot
characters.description: A robot who was one of Coop's challengers in "The Return". He often spoke in a mean rhyming tone saying stuff as "T-Bot will pounce you! Trounce you! And utterly denounce you!". Determinator: No matter how much he's beaten up he'll just come back for more, even at the end after gettingcut in halfhe's still going, dragging his legless torso along the ground. Monster of the Week: His only major role is in "The Return". Though he makes a cameo in the finale. No Celebrities Were Harmed: He's a parody of Mr T. Rhymes on a Dime: Although he starts to run out of them by the end.

characters.name: R.E.C.R.
characters.description: Short for Reverse-Engineered Collective Robot, it is a robotic war machine created by Area 50. Its prime programming was to defeat "the enemy", but due to a programming flaw, it considered everything to be that enemy. It was awakened by Coop and fed off of MEGAS' electrical energy to become stronger. After trapping MEGAS in Area 50, R.E.C.R went in search of power for its need to consume vast amounts of energy to operate properly. A.I. Is a Crapshoot: Its programming was simply to "destroy the enemy". Unfortunately, its "massive 56-kilobyte processor" didn't have much room for rationalizing who that was.Coop:I've got 20-year-old video games that are smarter than you. American Accents: He speaks in a stereotypical American accent that of a patriot soldier from the 20th century. Bootstrapped Leitmotif:"Ro-Sham-Bo"is often associated with him as it is prominently featured during his fights. Combining Mecha: Inverted, he's a robot that can separate into two tanks, two fighter jets and a UFO respectively.He can also do this to dodge attacks.

characters.name: Gynok
characters.description: An alien plant spore from Saturn. It requires heat to grow and germinate. Taking the disguise of Auggie the Adorable Aardvark (a spoof ofSonic the HedgehogandMickey Mouse), it attacked MEGAS because it considered it to be a threat to its germination process, later multiplying and taking the guises of the other Thanksgiving parade balloons. Be Careful What You Wish For: Enjoyed Coop blasting him with heat, until it ends up destroying him. Blob Monster: His initial form is a green blob of jelly. He changes into more combat-suitable forms once he realizes that his default form puts him at a significant disadvantage on Earth. Everyone Calls Him "Barkeep": Despite constantly referring to himself by name, Coop keeps calling him "Auggie" since that was the form he initially appeared in. Monster of the Week: He only appears in "Thanksgiving Throwdown".

characters.name: Zarek
characters.description: Another of the S-Force's enemies and according to Coop, is a "pointy-headed chump". He captures planet Sarus and tried to throw the S-Force into the Infinity Zone. Just in time, Coop came to their rescue. Expy: Of Berg Katse (aka "Zoltar" and "Galactor", as he is known in the dubs), a major enemy of theScience Ninja Team Gatchaman. Horrifying the Horror: Refers to Megas as a "metal monstrosity" after Coop singlehandedly destroys all his guard bots. Kneel Before Zod: Demands this from the denizens of Sarus after defeating the S-Force. Large Ham: Courtesy of Jeff Bennett.

characters.name: Captain Warlock
characters.description: An evil space pirate and captain of the pirate battleship Saving Grace. Warlock has a fetish for red hair and he unsurprisingly fell in love with Kiva. He tricked her by promising that he would give her a time drive and attempted to trap by and threatening her with the lives of Coop and Jamie to become his queen. Casanova Wannabe: Fancies himself this. Kiva's final complaint when she hijacks Coop's "roll" was having to listen to his "endless, cheesy pickup lines".Warlock:Cheesy!? Exact Words: Lets Coop and Jamie go as promised, but purposely leaves them in a situation that will probably get them killed.Warlock: Come now, there's a very good chance they'llsurvivethe impact. Of course, they'll be in no shape to fight off the lorgnarts if they do. Expy: OfCaptain Harlock. Fake Ultimate Hero: He acts like a roguish space pirate, but he's actually an outright villain. His claim to have defeated hundreds of the vicious Lorgnarts is also highly suspect, as his main defences appear to consist of hiding behind a cloaking shield and deploying a force of mechs to do all the fighting for him.

characters.name: Drallag
characters.description: Leader of the selfish and greedy Cyrellians. Because the Cyrellians require water for their coolant systems, they drained their planet's supply of water and became focused on the ice moon nearby. Cyber Cyclops: The rank and file soldiers are all monoeyes. Expy: The Cyrellians are parodies of the Decepticons thus making Drallag an expy of Megatron. The minions of the army are also based on Shockwave. Hoist by His Own Petard: He was tricked by Coop into accidentally destroying his home planet while attempting to destroy him with his flagship's cutter laser. On top of that, he's killed when his ship is destroyed by the remnants of the planet. Monster of the Week: He only appears in "Ice Ice Megas".

characters.name: Klaar
characters.description: The head of the Omnicron One Maximum Security Prison and Artificial Intelligence Rehabilitation Facility. Klaar reprograms the A.I.'s of dangerous criminal robots for the greater good. He mistook MEGAS for one of these, but the misunderstanding was soon cleared up. Klaar decided to destroy MEGAS anyway due to Coop's interference. Ultimately, the criminals were all released thanks to Coop, who thought the robots were being held as slaves by Klaar. Brainwashing for the Greater Good: How he deals with some of the "most dangerous criminals" in the galaxy. Mistaken Identity: He assumes Megas XLR was one of the prisoners due to the Number 12 designation it sported and attempted to brainwash it. Unfortunately, it only works on sentient giant robots, leaving Coop and his friends unaffected. Offscreen Moment of Awesome: If the end credits scene of one of the robot criminals happily skipping away as it was about to fight Megas is any indication, he somehow repaired the satellite that was used to reprogram the robotic prisoners after Coop destroyed it. One-Shot Character: He only appears in "A Clockwork Megas".

characters.name: Skalgar
characters.description: An intergalactic robotic crimial who speaks in a Spanish accent. Skalgar strives to be the most feared criminal in the universe, but because of his size he is often made fun of. In spite of this, he is incredibly powerful. When he gets word of a universal controller made by Coop, he goes to claim it for his own thinking it will let him control the universe. Accidental Misnaming: Coop ends up calling him "school girl" and Jamie later does it deliberately to goad him into firing into hisDeflector ShieldsCoop had inadvertently reversed the polarity of. Berserk Button: Laughing at or not taking him seriously,especiallyif it involves mocking his height. Deflector Shields: Accidentally gains this on his mech after Coop hits him with the mazer refractor (that he repaired incorrectly). Dude, Where's My Respect?: He complains about the measly bounty on his head, despite how dangerous he truly is and he is mocked for his size.

characters.name: Alternate Jamie
characters.description: The leader of the resistance force in an alternate dimension. He is the hero of that Earth who fights against the evil forces of Coop and his army of robots. He apparently had a relationship with Kiva, but she left with Coop and became evil. Alternate Jamie is a lovelorn, battle-hardened man with a knee brace. This version of Jamie is actually the polar opposite of the original Jamie, being very brave and fit. Ace Pilot: Has become this over the course of the war. The Apocalypse Brings Out the Best in People: Who would have thought Coop and Kiva pulling aFace–Heel Turnand going on a rampage across the universe would be what it took for Jamie to shed his cowardice and weakness to become a genuine hero? Baritone of Strength: He is the leader of the resistance against Evil Coop and he has a deep voice, showing how different he was from his counterpart when Coop meets him. Call-Back: Alternate Jamie piloting his custom Glorft mech is this toUltra Chickswhere his counterpart thought he would be using the war mech filled with various weaponry before he was given a different one to his dismay.

characters.name: Alternate Coop
characters.description: An evil counterpart of Coop from an alternate dimension who became a ruthless warlord who laid waste to his home dimension through his obsession with destroying everything in his path and would like to do it all over again, and he pilots a more powerful warlike mech after abandoning his own Megas. 13 Is Unlucky: A bad guy with the number thirteen painted on his armor. Ax-Crazy: He's prone to cackling like a maniac whenever he's fighting and commits wanton destruction simply for his own amusement. Be Careful What You Wish For: He plans to conquer dimensions to satisfy his excessive blood lust only to wind up facing off against its denizens who prove to be far harder than he could handle. Bigger Stick: His red mech is this as Evil Coop uses it to easily manhandle Megas, both the original and his old one he stashed away, and possessed a variety of powerful weapons such as theRocket Punch,Arm Cannonand a red blade.

characters.name: Alternate Kiva
characters.description: An evil counterpart of Kiva from an alternate dimension who became Evil Coop's second-in-command and is more machine than woman, twisted and evil after losing her own humanity. She pilots her former Earth Coalition mech she repaired and modified to carry out Evil Coop's will to destroy everything. Cybernetics Eat Your Soul: After becoming Coop's Dragon, her skin turned a pale gray and she was outfitted with cybernetic implants that apparently keep her hooked up to her mecha since she is never once seen outside of it throughout the episode. The Dark Side Will Make You Forget: Initially, Evil Kiva followed her Coop as a means to get back to the future and continue the fight for humanity. Eventually, she lost sight of her goal and became truly loyal to him. Deadpan Snarker: Despite being cold and more machine than woman, she still has some shades of this as when she's unimpressed of Coop's old MEGAS already failing to show that it's not outdated when it actually is. The Dragon: She's the main minion to Evil Coop and she help command his armies to destroy everything.
